meta.purpose: Authority repair integration for care-delay, incontinence care, staffing, retaliation, and gross neglect context after source-locked QA
meta.normalization_note: This batch preserves conservative authority bridges from the repair pass while aligning them to the app's current ACL/NORC Table 2 hierarchy, where F08 is Incontinence care and F06 is Access to health-related services.

authority_rows.authority_label: Quality of life - activities of daily living
authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: Current authority-repair QA anchors incontinence and toileting support to the ADL assistance requirement in §483.24(a)(2) rather than to a single Appendix PP tag.
authority_rows.human_review_note: Use this CFR as the primary federal context for incontinence and toileting care. Surface Appendix PP tags separately only when explicitly supported by current CMS guidance.

authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: Retaliation complaints are best anchored to the grievance-rights subsection protecting the resident's ability to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal.
authority_rows.human_review_note: Use the grievance-without-reprisal subsection as the main federal context for D06. Do not imply that a single Appendix PP tag controls every retaliation complaint.

authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: Current authority-repair QA keeps J03 tied to the sufficient nursing staff requirement in §483.35 and treats Appendix PP staffing tags as contextual rather than mandatory.
authority_rows.human_review_note: Use the CFR staffing requirement as the main authority anchor. Treat F725 or other staffing tags as additional context when they are helpful, not as required crosswalk outputs.

authority_rows.authority_label: Freedom from abuse, neglect, and exploitation
authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: Gross neglect complaints should stay anchored to the federal abuse and neglect protections in §483.12, with more specific care codes added separately when the facts support multiple complaints.
authority_rows.human_review_note: Use A05 only when the facts meet NORS gross-neglect criteria. Specific care failures such as response delay or incontinence care may still be coded separately when the resident identifies them as distinct complaints.

source_conflict_rows.issue: Incoming authority-repair handoff used F06 for toileting and incontinence care, but the current app hierarchy and official ACL/NORC Table 2 place incontinence care under F08.
source_conflict_rows.source_a: ACL/NORC Table 2 identifies F08 as Incontinence care and F06 as Access to health-related services.
source_conflict_rows.source_b: Incoming repair handoff framed toileting and incontinence authority bridges under F06.
source_conflict_rows.conflict_description: Importing the handoff literally would attach incontinence-care logic to the wrong current NORS code.
source_conflict_rows.recommended_resolution: Keep F02 for response to requests for assistance, F08 for incontinence care, J03 for staffing, D06 for retaliation, and A05 for gross neglect. Re-express any F06 toileting authority rows as F08 before production use.
source_conflict_rows.human_review_note: This conflict concerns the current code numbering, not the usefulness of the underlying toileting-care authority logic.

tooltip_rows.label: Response Delay Authority
tooltip_rows.short_help: F02 does not have one uniquely controlling Appendix PP tag in the approved source set.
tooltip_rows.details: Code F02 from NORS first. Quality-of-care or staffing authorities can help explain context, but the tool should not imply that one F-tag conclusively controls every call-light or response-delay complaint.

tooltip_rows.label: F02 vs F08 vs A05
tooltip_rows.short_help: Separate response delay, incontinence care, and gross neglect based on facts, pattern, and harm.
tooltip_rows.details: Use F02 for unanswered requests for help, F08 when the core issue is incontinence or toileting care, and review A05 only when repeated essential-care failures threaten health or safety.

human_review_flags.issue: No single Appendix PP tag was confirmed as the uniquely controlling authority for F02 response-to-assistance complaints in the approved source set.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Keep F02 coding grounded in ACL/NORC Table 2. Use quality-of-care or staffing authorities as contextual review support only, and avoid implying that one F-tag conclusively governs every response-delay complaint.

human_review_flags.issue: Boundary between F02 response delay, F08 incontinence care, and A05 gross neglect still depends on resident framing, duration, and harm.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: When notes describe unanswered calls plus being left wet or soiled, decide whether the resident is raising one concern or multiple distinct complaints. Escalate to A05 only when repeated essential-care failures threaten health or safety.
